briefdescription.para: Counter count up flag.
Flag preventing counter reset when top value is changed.

detaileddescription.para: If flags is set then counter is free running while top value is updated, otherwise counter is reset (see counter_set_top_value()).

briefdescription.para: Flag instructing counter to reset itself if changing top value results in counter going out of new top value bound.

detaileddescription.para: See COUNTER_TOP_CFG_DONT_RESET.

briefdescription.para: Counter alarm absolute value flag.

detaileddescription.para: Ticks relation to counter value. If set ticks are treated as absolute value, else it is relative to the counter reading performed during the call.

briefdescription.para: Alarm flag enabling immediate expiration when driver detects that absolute alarm was set too late.

detaileddescription.para: Alarm callback must be called from the same context as if it was set on time.

briefdescription.para: Identifies guard period needed for detection of late setting of absolute alarm (see counter_set_channel_alarm).

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* counter_alarm_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, uint32_t ticks, void *user_data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, uint32_t ticks, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: counter_alarm_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Alarm callback.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Channel ID.
Counter value that triggered the alarm.
User data.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* counter_top_callback_t) (const struct device *dev, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: counter_top_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Callback called when counter turns around.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
User data provided in counter_set_top_value.

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* counter_alarm_callback_64_t) (const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, uint64_t ticks, void *user_data)
memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, uint64_t ticks, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: counter_alarm_callback_64_t

briefdescription.para: Alarm callback.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Channel ID.
Counter value that triggered the alarm.
User data.

briefdescription.para: Function to check if counter is counting up.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
if counter is counting up.
if counter is counting down.

briefdescription.para: Function to get number of alarm channels.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Function to get counter frequency in 64bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: Frequency of the counter in Hz, or zero if the counter does not have a fixed frequency.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert microseconds to ticks.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Microseconds.

simplesect.para: Converted ticks. Ticks will be saturated if exceed 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert microseconds to ticks with 64 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Microseconds.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert ticks to microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Ticks.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert ticks with 64 bits to microseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Ticks in 64 bits.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert nanoseconds to ticks.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Nanoseconds.

simplesect.para: Converted ticks. Ticks will be saturated if exceed 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert nanoseconds to ticks with 64 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Nanoseconds.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert ticks to nanoseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Ticks.

briefdescription.para: Function to convert ticks with 64 bits to nanoseconds.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Ticks in 64 bits.

briefdescription.para: Function to retrieve maximum top value that can be set.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

simplesect.para: With CONFIG_COUNTER_64BITS_TICKS enabled this function returns only the lower 32 bits of the max top value. Use counter_get_max_top_value_64() to get the full 64 bit value if it is expected to exceed 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: Start counter device in free running mode.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful.
Negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Stop counter device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful.
if the device doesn't support stopping the counter.

briefdescription.para: Get current counter value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to where to store the current counter value
If successful.
Negative error code on failure getting the counter value

briefdescription.para: Reset the counter to the initial value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
If successful.
Negative error code on failure resetting the counter value.

briefdescription.para: Set current counter value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Tick value to set
If successful.
error code on failure setting the counter value

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, const struct counter_alarm_cfg *alarm_cfg)
memberdef.name: counter_set_channel_alarm

briefdescription.para: Set a single shot alarm on a channel.

detaileddescription.para: After expiration alarm can be set again, disabling is not needed. When alarm expiration handler is called, channel is considered available and can be set again in that context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Channel ID.
Alarm configuration.
If successful.
if request is not supported (device does not support interrupts or requested channel).
if alarm settings are invalid.
if absolute alarm was set too late.
if alarm is already active.

briefdescription.para: Cancel an alarm on a channel.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Channel ID.
If successful.
if request is not supported or the counter was not started yet.

briefdescription.para: Set counter top value.

detaileddescription.para: Function sets top value and optionally resets the counter to 0 or top value depending on counter direction. On turnaround, counter can be reset and optional callback is periodically called. Top value can only be changed when there is no active channel alarm.

COUNTER_TOP_CFG_DONT_RESET prevents counter reset. When counter is running while top value is updated, it is possible that counter progresses outside the new top value. In that case, error is returned and optionally driver can reset the counter (see COUNTER_TOP_CFG_RESET_WHEN_LATE).

simplesect.para: With CONFIG_COUNTER_64BITS_TICKS enabled this function sets the lower 32 bits of the max top value. Use counter_set_top_value_64() to get the full 64 bit value if it is expected to exceed 32 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Configuration. Cannot be NULL.
If successful.
if request is not supported (e.g. top value cannot be changed or counter cannot/must be reset during top value update).
if any alarm is active.
if COUNTER_TOP_CFG_DONT_RESET was set and new top value is smaller than current counter value (counter counting up).

briefdescription.para: Function to get pending interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: The purpose of this function is to return the interrupt status register for the device. This is especially useful when waking up from low power states to check the wake up source.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
if any counter interrupt is pending.
if no counter interrupt is pending.

briefdescription.para: Function to retrieve current top value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint32_t ticks, uint32_t flags)
memberdef.name: counter_set_guard_period

briefdescription.para: Set guard period in counter ticks.

detaileddescription.para: When setting an absolute alarm value close to the current counter value there is a risk that the counter will have counted past the given absolute value before the driver manages to activate the alarm. If this would go unnoticed then the alarm would only expire after the timer has wrapped and reached the given absolute value again after a full timer period. This could take a long time in case of a 32 bit timer. Setting a sufficiently large guard period will help the driver detect unambiguously whether it is late or not.

The guard period should be as many counter ticks as the driver will need at most to actually activate the alarm after the driver API has been called. If the driver finds that the counter has just passed beyond the given absolute tick value but is still close enough to fall within the guard period, it will assume that it is "late", i.e. that the intended expiry time has already passed. Depending on the COUNTER_ALARM_CFG_EXPIRE_WHEN_LATE flag the driver will either ignore the alarm or expire it immediately in such a case.

If, however, the counter is past the given absolute tick value but outside the guard period, then the driver will assume that this is intentional and let the counter wrap around to/from zero before it expires.
More precisely:

listitem.para: When counting upwards (see COUNTER_CONFIG_INFO_COUNT_UP) the given absolute tick value must be above (now + guard_period) % top_value to be accepted by the driver.
When counting downwards, the given absolute tick value must be less than (now + top_value - guard_period) % top_value to be accepted.

detaileddescription.para: Examples:

listitem.para: counting upwards, now = 4950, top value = 5000, guard period = 100: absolute tick value >= (4950 + 100) % 5000 = 50
counting downwards, now = 50, top value = 5000, guard period = 100: absolute tick value <= (50 + 5000 - * 100) % 5000 = 4950

detaileddescription.para: If you need only short alarm periods, you can set the guard period very high (e.g. half of the counter top value) which will make it highly unlikely that the counter will ever unintentionally wrap.
The guard period is set to 0 on initialization (no protection).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Guard period in counter ticks.
See COUNTER_GUARD_PERIOD_FLAGS.
if successful.
if function or flags are not supported.
if ticks value is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Return guard period.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
See COUNTER_GUARD_PERIOD_FLAGS.

simplesect.para: Guard period given in counter ticks or 0 if function or flags are not supported.

briefdescription.para: Function to retrieve maximum top value that can be set for 64 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Set counter top value for 64 bits.

detaileddescription.para: Function sets top value and optionally resets the counter to 0 or top value depending on counter direction. On turnaround, counter can be reset and optional callback is periodically called. Top value can only be changed when there is no active channel alarm.

COUNTER_TOP_CFG_DONT_RESET prevents counter reset. When counter is running while top value is updated, it is possible that counter progresses outside the new top value. In that case, error is returned and optionally driver can reset the counter (see COUNTER_TOP_CFG_RESET_WHEN_LATE).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Configuration. Cannot be NULL.
If successful.
if request is not supported (e.g. top value cannot be changed or counter cannot/must be reset during top value update).
if any alarm is active.
if COUNTER_TOP_CFG_DONT_RESET was set and new top value is smaller than current counter value (counter counting up).

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint8_t chan_id, const struct counter_alarm_cfg_64 *alarm_cfg)
memberdef.name: counter_set_channel_alarm_64

briefdescription.para: Set a single shot alarm on a channel for 64 bits.

detaileddescription.para: After expiration alarm can be set again, disabling is not needed. When alarm expiration handler is called, channel is considered available and can be set again in that context.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Channel ID.
Alarm configuration.
If successful.
if request is not supported (device does not support interrupts or requested channel).
if alarm settings are invalid.
if absolute alarm was set too late.
if alarm is already active.

briefdescription.para: Function to retrieve current top value for 64 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, uint64_t ticks, uint32_t flags)
memberdef.name: counter_set_guard_period_64

briefdescription.para: Set guard period in counter ticks for 64 bits.

detaileddescription.para: When setting an absolute alarm value close to the current counter value there is a risk that the counter will have counted past the given absolute value before the driver manages to activate the alarm. If this would go unnoticed then the alarm would only expire after the timer has wrapped and reached the given absolute value again after a full timer period. This could take a long time in case of a 32 bit timer. Setting a sufficiently large guard period will help the driver detect unambiguously whether it is late or not.

The guard period should be as many counter ticks as the driver will need at most to actually activate the alarm after the driver API has been called. If the driver finds that the counter has just passed beyond the given absolute tick value but is still close enough to fall within the guard period, it will assume that it is "late", i.e. that the intended expiry time has already passed. Depending on the COUNTER_ALARM_CFG_EXPIRE_WHEN_LATE flag the driver will either ignore the alarm or expire it immediately in such a case.

If, however, the counter is past the given absolute tick value but outside the guard period, then the driver will assume that this is intentional and let the counter wrap around to/from zero before it expires.
More precisely:

listitem.para: When counting upwards (see COUNTER_CONFIG_INFO_COUNT_UP) the given absolute tick value must be above (now + guard_period) % top_value to be accepted by the driver.
When counting downwards, the given absolute tick value must be less than (now + top_value - guard_period) % top_value to be accepted.

detaileddescription.para: Examples:

listitem.para: counting upwards, now = 4950, top value = 5000, guard period = 100: absolute tick value >= (4950 + 100) % 5000 = 50
counting downwards, now = 50, top value = 5000, guard period = 100: absolute tick value <= (50 + 5000 - * 100) % 5000 = 4950

detaileddescription.para: If you need only short alarm periods, you can set the guard period very high (e.g. half of the counter top value) which will make it highly unlikely that the counter will ever unintentionally wrap.
The guard period is set to 0 on initialization (no protection).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Guard period in counter ticks of 64 bits.
See COUNTER_GUARD_PERIOD_FLAGS.
if successful.
if function or flags are not supported.
if ticks value is invalid.

briefdescription.para: Return guard period for 64 bits.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
See COUNTER_GUARD_PERIOD_FLAGS.

simplesect.para: Guard period given in counter ticks or 0 if function or flags are not supported.

briefdescription.para: Get current counter 64-bit value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Pointer to where to store the current counter value in 64 bits.
If successful.
Negative error code on failure getting the counter value

briefdescription.para: Set current counter 64-bit value.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for the driver instance.
Tick value to set in 64 bits
If successful.
Negative error code on failure setting the counter value

briefdescription.para: Interfaces for counters.
